title: Cured Jamón Hanging Under Warm Industrial Lighting in a Traditional Buenos Aires Deli Market
description: Buenos Aires, Buenos Aires: Strings of traditional Jamón Torgelon hang from the ceiling of a historic market, their white protective mesh casings catching the warm, directional glow of overhead track lighting. The composition utilizes a low-angle perspective, emphasizing the repetitive geometry of the cured meats against the structural support beams of the ceiling. Each label on the cloth coverings stands out in sharp focus, offering a glimpse into the artisanal food production practices that define local porteño gastronomy. The interplay between the artificial light and the neutral, tactile fabric of the ham wrappings creates a high-contrast visual experience that evokes the sensory richness of an old-world deli. As the lights illuminate the hanging bounty, the surrounding shadows recede, focusing the viewer’s attention on the rustic simplicity of the cured process. The atmosphere is one of deliberate preservation and heritage, suggesting a place where culinary customs are protected and proudly displayed for both locals and travelers alike. Exploring such traditional markets allows one to peel back the layers of Argentine culinary history, revealing the foundational role that specialized production techniques play in maintaining the cultural identity of the city. These spaces serve as vital conduits for community engagement, preserving flavors that have been perfected over generations. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
